titleStmt.title: SII 31.75: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 75. (A.R. No. B 272 of 1936.) INJĒḌU, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up against the prākāra wall (inside) to the proper left of the main entrance into the temple of Veṅkaṭēśvara.
This is dated Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Māgha, śu. 15, Thursday Lunar echlipse corresponding to January 29, 1534 A.D.

It records the gift of marriage tax (peṇdlisunkamu) mūlavīsālu called ādimārgam probably collected from the village Iñjēḍu for maintaining the food offerings and other services to the god Tiruvangaḷanātha by Rāmappa, son of Būcharasa for the merit of his parents, on the occasion of lunar eclipse. The village Iñjēḍu is stated to have been situated in the Guddalūrusīma, the nāyaṁkara by Bāchrasayya, the sthānāpati of the king Achyutadēvarāya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
su(śu)bhamastu . svasti . śrī jayā
dbu(bhyu)daya śālivāhana śakavaru
ṣaṁbyu(bu)lu 1455 Agunēṭi
vijaya saṁvvatsara māga su(śu) 15 gu .
śrī manmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśva
ra śrīvīrapratāpa . śrīvīra Acyuta rā
ya mahārāyalu prithivī rājyaṁ
śēyu cuṁḍḍi . stānāpati bācarasa
yyavāriki nāyaṁkrānaku pāliṁcci
na guddalūri śīmalōniki cellē yiṁjē
ṭi tiruveṁgaḷanāṁthaniki bācara
sayyavāri komaruṁḍu rāma
ppaṁgāru tama tallidaṁḍṟlakuṁ
buṇyaṁgānu yiṁjēṭinu celli
vaccē peṁḍlisuṁkaṁ Iṁjēṭi tiruveṁgaḷa
nāṁdaniki Āmṛtapaḷaṁkunnu naivē
dya paṁnnerālakuṁnnu Aṁggaraṁgga
vaibhavālakuṁnnu sōmagraha
ṇa puṇyakālāna yiṁjēṭi peṁḍli
suṁkaṁ Ādimāgaṇa mūlavīsā
lu saḥ dhāravosi yistiṁ yiccina tharma
śāsanaḥ . yidi naḍapinavāriki padi
vēlu puṇyāluddhisiddhaṁcunu . yiṁdduku
tharmmānaku yavaru kīḍu dalaṁcina
nu . tama talidaṁḍṟlānu guruvu
lānu . vāraṇāsilōjaṁppina pāpā
naṁbōvuvāru . svadattā dvīguṁ
puṇyaṁ paradattānu pālanaṁ . paradattā
paharēṇa svadataṁ niṣpalaṁ bhavētī .
dhāravōyiṁccina tarimela si
ṁgajula koḍuku virmarājuku puṇyaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 31.75 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
